review_notes: The functions provided are generally well-aligned with the control SC-7(25) for boundary protection in Kubernetes. They focus on network policies and service configurations that prevent unauthorized access, which is crucial for boundary protection. The naming conventions are mostly followed, but some improvements in clarity and specificity could be made.
naming_improvements: Consider renaming 'k8s_service_type_not_nodeport' to 'k8s_service_nodeport_disabled' for better clarity and consistency with pass/fail indication.
title: Boundary Protection - Boundary protection | unclassified national security system connections prohibit
step1_reasoning: The control SC-7(25) focuses on boundary protection by prohibiting unclassified national security system connections. In a Kubernetes context, this translates to ensuring that network boundaries are protected by default-deny NetworkPolicies for both ingress and egress traffic. Additionally, services should not be exposed using NodePort, which can allow external access. Enabling TLS on Ingress resources ensures secure connections, further protecting the boundary.
